flashcards.front: Define a coset in group theory
flashcards.back: A coset of a subgroup H in group G is the set obtained by multiplying all elements of H by a fixed element a from G. It is denoted as aH = {ah : h ∈ H} for left cosets.
The left coset of subgroup H with representative a in group G is the set aH = {ah | h ∈ H}. It contains all elements obtained by multiplying a on the left with each element of H.
The right coset of subgroup H with representative a in group G is the set Ha = {ha | h ∈ H}. It contains all elements obtained by multiplying each element of H by a on the right.

flashcards.front: Define the index of a subgroup
flashcards.back: The index of subgroup H in group G, denoted [G : H], is the number of distinct left cosets of H in G. It equals |G|/|H| when G is finite.
If H is a subgroup of a finite group G, then the order of H divides the order of G, and |G| = [G : H] × |H|.
A subgroup N of group G is normal (denoted N ◁ G) if aN = Na for all a ∈ G, meaning left and right cosets coincide.
The quotient group G/N consists of cosets of normal subgroup N in G, with operation (aN)(bN) = (ab)N. Denoted as G/N.

flashcards.front: How do cosets partition a group?
flashcards.back: Cosets of a subgroup H in G form a partition of G - they are disjoint and their union equals G. Any two cosets are either identical or have empty intersection.
Two left cosets aH and bH are equal if and only if a⁻¹b ∈ H (or equivalently, b ∈ aH). This is the coset equality criterion.
A coset representative is any element used to name a coset. For coset aH, 'a' is the representative. Different representatives may yield the same coset.
The kernel of a group homomorphism φ: G → G' is the set {g ∈ G | φ(g) = e'}, the preimage of identity. It is a normal subgroup of G.

flashcards.front: What is the First Isomorphism Theorem?
flashcards.back: If φ: G → G' is a homomorphism with kernel K, then G/K ≅ φ(G). The quotient group G/K is isomorphic to the image of φ.

flashcards.front: Are all cosets the same size as the subgroup?
flashcards.back: Yes, each coset of subgroup H has exactly |H| elements. There is a one-to-one correspondence between any coset and H itself.
For normal subgroup N, coset multiplication is defined as (aN)(bN) = (ab)N. This operation is well-defined only when N is normal.

flashcards.front: What is the trivial subgroup's coset?
flashcards.back: The trivial subgroup {e} has cosets that are just singleton sets {a} for each a ∈ G. The index equals |G|.
